description_long: This is a small, modern civilian airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The wings are slightly swept and feature a moderate dihedral angle, with no visible winglets. The aircraft has a V-tail design, which is distinctive and less common than conventional tails, and a single engine mounted on top of the rear fuselage. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a green-tinted canopy, featuring multiple windows for visibility. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with a single nose wheel and two main wheels, all of which appear to be fixed and non-retractable.
description: A compact, low-wing civilian airplane with a V-tail, rear-mounted engine, and tricycle landing gear. The cockpit is forward and enclosed with green-tinted windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the low-mounted, slightly swept wings, V-tail, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, three cockpit windows, and the rear-mounted engine above the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the V-tail configuration, engine placement, and the symmetry of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit windows, landing gear, and the distinctive V-tail and engine mount.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, slightly swept with moderate dihedral angle, no winglets.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle, fixed, with one nose wheel and two main wheels
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multi-seat configuration and green-tinted windows
